title: 6 Posting AFT Service Charges
body_text:
6	Posting AFT Service Charges
6.1	Posting AFT Service Charges
AFT Transaction Service Charges
Outgoing Transaction Charges â Outgoing Canadian and US dollar AFT transaction charges are posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account as code OF at month-end. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725, for AFT service charges. If it is within the bounds of the financial institutionâs agreement with the business member, proceed as follows to post the charges to an originatorâs account:
â
Determine the total number of transactions per originator by using the Monthly Customer AFT Billing Report (ICCB/UCCB) and/or the Monthly AFT Transaction Volumes Report (ICTV/UCTV). See the AFT Reports Part for more information on these reports.
â
Manually post the total to the originatorâs financial institution account at month-end.
Incoming Transaction Charges â Incoming AFT transaction charges are posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account at month-end as code DD (credits) or code PP (debits). Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725, for AFT service charges. Financial institutions that decide to pass on the fees to their members must determine how to do so. For example, the fee can be included as part of the memberâs account fee.
Fees for AFT Service
Originators that automatically release AFT files on
PaymentStream
AFT are subject to a monthly AFT service fee. Originators that upload AFT files to
PaymentStream
AFT or the File and Report Exchange or that manually release files on
PaymentStream
AFT are subject to a per file charge. These fees are in addition to the transaction charges described above. They are reported as follows:
AFT Monthly Fee Report (AFTM/UFTM) â Lists the monthly service charge associated with each originator that is set up to automatically release AFT files on
PaymentStream
AFT. It is sorted by branch.
AFT Clients File Fee Monthly Summary (AFMS/UFMS) â Lists the total file charges associated with each Originator ID per month. These charges apply to originators that upload AFT files to
PaymentStream
AFT or the File and Report Exchange or that manually release files on
PaymentStream
AFT. It is sorted by branch.
AFT Clients Daily Charges Report (AFTD/UFTD) â Lists the total file charges associated with each Originator ID per day. These charges apply to originators that upload AFT files to
PaymentStream
AFT or the File and Report Exchange or that manually release files on
PaymentStream
AFT. It is sorted by branch.
Monthly Customer AFT Billing Report (ICCB/UCCB)
â Lists the monthly billing for consolidated transactions associated with the Originator ID. The report also provides details of transaction volumes, returns/rejects, and
PaymentStream
AFT user fees (file uploads and monthly user fee).
Monthly AFT Transaction Volumes Report (ICTV/UCTV)
â
Lists the monthly volumes for Credit Clearing, AFT Return, and AFT Originating transactions. The report provides information to determine the total number of transactions per originator.
Note:	See the AFT Reports Part for more information on these reports.
These charges are posted to the financial institutionâs account with Central 1. If applicable, the financial institution uses the above reports to manually post the charges to the originatorsâ accounts.
Set-Up Fees
Each new originator is subject to a one-time fee for the set-up of AFT service. These fees are posted to the credit unionâs Central 1 account. Credit unions can recover the set-up cost by manually posting the fee to the business memberâs account.
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
END
